notes: Covers paid firefighters, paid fire fighting instructors, sworn members of Office of State Fire Marshal employed by airport authority, county, fire control district, municipality, or State. Hypertension sub-provision (§9-503(a)(2)) requires: hypertension diagnosis by authorized provider, medication prescribed for at least 90 consecutive days, at least 2 years cumulative service, and currently employed at time of claim — effective October 1, 2026.
Covers paid firefighters, paid fire fighting instructors, sworn members of Office of State Fire Marshal employed by airport authority, county, fire control district, municipality, or State.

Covers paid rescue squad members and paid advanced life support unit members. Hypertension sub-provision (§9-503(a)(2)) requires: diagnosis by authorized provider, medication for at least 90 consecutive days, at least 2 years cumulative service, and currently employed at time of claim — effective October 1, 2026.
Covers paid rescue squad members and paid advanced life support unit members.

Covers volunteer firefighters, volunteer fire fighting instructors, volunteer rescue squad members, and volunteer advanced life support unit members who are covered employees under § 9-234. Pre-employment physical exam required. Hypertension expanded provision (§9-503(a)(2)) applies only to paid members.
Covers volunteer rescue squad members and volunteer advanced life support unit members who are covered employees under § 9-234. Pre-employment physical exam required.

condition_specific: leukemia, prostate, rectal, throat, multiple myeloma, non-Hodgkin's lymphoma, brain, testicular, bladder, kidney/renal cell, thyroid, colon, ovarian, breast cancer
notes: Requires: (1) cancer caused by contact with toxic substance encountered in the line of duty; (2) at least 10 years of service as firefighter, fire fighting instructor, rescue squad member, or advanced life support unit member; (3) unable to perform normal duties due to cancer. Covers paid firefighters, paid fire fighting instructors, and sworn members of Office of State Fire Marshal.

condition_specific: leukemia, prostate, rectal, throat, multiple myeloma, non-Hodgkin's lymphoma, brain, testicular, bladder, kidney/renal cell, thyroid, colon, ovarian, breast cancer
notes: Covers volunteer firefighters, volunteer fire fighting instructors, volunteer rescue squad members, and volunteer advanced life support unit members who are covered employees under § 9-234. Pre-employment physical exam required. Requires 10 years of service; cancer must be caused by toxic substance in line of duty; must be unable to perform normal duties.
